name: Laguna de San Marcos – running circular via the Camino de Las Raíces - Parque Natural de las Batuecas y Sierra de Francia
name_original: Laguna de San Marcos – running circular por el Camino de Las Raíces - Parque Natural de las Batuecas y Sierra de Francia
region_intro: Jogging around La Alberca offers a diverse landscape within the Sierra de Francia mountains and the Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park. The region features varied terrain, including extensive oak, chestnut, and walnut forests, as well as unique strawberry tree forests. Runners can explore river valleys with waterfalls, mountainous areas with significant elevation changes, and serene lagoons. This natural setting provides a range of running routes suitable for different preferences and fitness levels. Best jogging routes around La Alberca The most popular jogging route is Laguna de San Marcos – running circular via the Camino de Las Raíces, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 9 minutes to complete. This route follows the scenic Camino de las Raíces, blending nature with cultural installations and offering views of Peña de Francia. Another top favourite among local runners is Peña de Francia – trail run from the El Casarito recreational area, a Difficult 7.7 miles (12.4 km) path. This trail features significant elevation gain, leading towards the emblematic Peña de Francia and offering commanding views of the natural territory. Local runners also love the Chorro de las Batuecas via the Valle de las Batuecas Route, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail leading through the historic Valley of Las Batuecas, often completed in about 1 hour 14 minutes. Jogging around La Alberca is defined by dense woodlands, mountainous terrain, and tranquil river valleys. The network of running routes offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents. The routes in La Alberca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. Nearly 200 runners have used komoot to explore La Alberca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around La Alberca?
A: There are over 20 running routes around La Alberca, offering a diverse range of experiences within the Sierra de Francia mountains and the Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park. These include paths through dense forests, along river valleys, and up to scenic viewpoints.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in La Alberca?
A: The terrain around La Alberca is quite varied, ranging from extensive oak, chestnut, and walnut forests to unique strawberry tree forests. You'll find paths along tranquil river valleys, some with waterfalls, and mountainous areas with significant elevation changes, especially towards peaks like Peña de Francia. This provides a mix of challenging ascents and more moderate paths.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993645586/laguna-de-san-marcos-running-circular-via-the-camino-de-las-raices-parque-natural-de-las-batuecas-y-sierra-de-francia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de San Marcos – running circular via the Camino de Las Raíces&lt;/a&gt;. This 9.3 km trail largely follows the scenic Camino de las Raíces, blending nature with cultural installations and offering views of Peña de Francia.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or natural features I can see while running near La Alberca?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Marcos Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy views of the emblematic Peña de Francia, and explore the historic Valley of Las Batuecas with its river and waterfalls. The area is also home to unique strawberry tree forests and dense woodlands of walnuts, chestnuts, and oaks. For panoramic views, consider routes that ascend towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1260027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de Francia&lt;/a&gt; itself.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier jog?
A: While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in our current selection, many of the 'moderate' routes can be suitable for beginners or those seeking a less strenuous experience, depending on their fitness level. These routes typically have less extreme elevation changes compared to the 'difficult' trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993653694/chorro-de-las-batuecas-via-the-valle-de-las-batuecas-route-batuecas-and-sierra-de-francia-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de las Batuecas via the Valle de las Batuecas Route&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.3 km trail through a tranquil valley.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in La Alberca?
A: The running routes in La Alberca are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, the refreshing environment of the natural park, and the variety of trails that cater to different fitness levels, from moderate paths through forests to challenging ascents with commanding views.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails around La Alberca?
A: Many natural parks and outdoor trails in Spain, including the Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park, generally permit dogs, often requiring them to be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the comfort of other visitors. It's always advisable to check specific regulations for individual trails or protected areas before you go.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the Valley of Las Batuecas is known for its natural beauty, including the Batuecas River which forms numerous waterfalls and crystalline pools. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993653694/chorro-de-las-batuecas-via-the-valle-de-las-batuecas-route-batuecas-and-sierra-de-francia-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de las Batuecas via the Valle de las Batuecas Route&lt;/a&gt; is a great option to experience this tranquil setting.

Q: What is the typical length of running trails in La Alberca?
A: The running trails in La Alberca vary in length. Many popular routes are between 9 km and 12.5 km. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993645586/laguna-de-san-marcos-running-circular-via-the-camino-de-las-raices-parque-natural-de-las-batuecas-y-sierra-de-francia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de San Marcos circular route&lt;/a&gt; is about 9.3 km, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993670418/pena-carbonera-and-pena-del-huevo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Carbonera and Peña del Huevo Trail&lt;/a&gt; extends to about 12.4 km.

Q: Are there any challenging trail runs for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993655617/pena-de-francia-trail-run-from-the-el-casarito-recreational-area-batuecas-natural-park-and-sierra-de-francia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de Francia – trail run from the El Casarito recreational area&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain. This 12.4 km path leads towards the iconic Peña de Francia, providing a strenuous workout and rewarding panoramic views of the natural territory.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in La Alberca?
A: La Alberca offers a refreshing environment for jogging throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially in the shaded forest areas and river valleys, though it's best to run in the mornings or late afternoons to avoid the midday heat. The village itself is adorned with geraniums in summer, adding to its charm.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest near the running routes?
A: Beyond the natural landscapes, La Alberca itself is a historic village, declared a National Historic Monument. Its narrow, picturesque streets with traditional half-timbered houses offer a unique cultural experience. The Camino de las Raíces, which some running routes follow, also features artistic installations along the way, blending nature with local culture and heritage.
tips: One of the most beautiful towns in Spain with a very particular charm, one of those towns that deserves to be visited and explore its picturesque streets. Located in the heart of the Sierra de Francia Park, the town also offers many options for trails and paths to explore this beautiful Natural Park.
Just 4 km from the beautiful town of La Alberca and within the Sierra de Francia Park is the San Marcos lagoon, a very quiet paradise surrounded by a wonderful landscape that is worth visiting.
